commit.message:
Harden CI workflows against supply chain attacks (#44)

* harden CI workflows against supply chain attacks

Pin all GitHub Actions to immutable commit SHAs instead of mutable
version tags, add explicit read-only permissions blocks, bump
setup-python v3→v5, and pin cfn-lint/checkov versions. Motivated by
the tj-actions/changed-files supply chain attack (CVE-2025-30066).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* address review: persist-credentials, shell injection, unpinned pip

- Add persist-credentials: false to all checkout steps so the
  GITHUB_TOKEN isn't left in git config for PR-controlled steps
- Replace unquoted command substitution with null-delimited
  git ls-files -z | xargs -0 to prevent filename injection in cfn-lint
- Drop pip install --upgrade pip to avoid an unpinned mutable
  execution path before pinned packages are installed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* debug lint workflow package resolution

Temporarily install cfn-lint and checkov without version pins and log the resolved versions so the next CI run shows what the runner can actually fetch. Keep the null-delimited template handling and explicit empty-list failure so shell safety and diagnosability remain intact.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* restore pinned lint tool versions

Reinstate the verified cfn-lint and checkov pins after confirming those exact versions install successfully in GitHub Actions. Keep the safer template file handling and explicit invariant check added during troubleshooting.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix typo in app README ("locahost" → "localhost")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add min-release-age=14 to .npmrc for supply chain safety

npm install will refuse to resolve package versions published less than
14 days ago. npm ci (used in CI) ignores this setting since it installs
from the lockfile. Override with --min-release-age=0 when needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify cfn-lint invocation to use shell glob directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update axios to 1.15.0 to resolve GHSA-3p68-rc4w-qgx5

proxy-from-env <2.0.0 allowed requests to bypass NO_PROXY restrictions.
axios 1.15.0 bumps proxy-from-env to ^2.1.0 which includes the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add dependabot for github actions

Scoped to github-actions only. Keeps SHA-pinned actions up to date
with a weekly check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve state bleeding in llm_limiter and improve rule_engine headers (#143)

* fix: parse comma-containing JWT claims in fallback decoder

* fix: loop detection warn action now sets X-Fairvisor-Warning header

When loop_detection.action is "warn", rule_engine._build_decision_from_loop
was checking loop_result.warning (a field never set by loop_detector.check)
to decide whether to emit the warning_code. This caused the X-Fairvisor-Warning
header to always be nil for the warn action.

Fix: use (loop_result.action == "warn") as the condition.

Adds a regression test: new scenario asserts that the warn action produces
an allow decision with X-Fairvisor-Warning: loop_warn header.

Fixes #141

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve state bleeding in llm_limiter and improve rule_engine headers

* test: restore coverage for limiter and parser fixes

* test: satisfy luacheck line length

commit.message:
ref: Replace ABOUT.md with WORLD.md, DESCRIPTION.md, and reference docs (#180)

Split ABOUT.md into separate files with concrete names based on how
they're
actually used in production (junior-prod):

- **SOUL.md** — personality/voice (unchanged)
- **WORLD.md** — operational/domain context, injected into system prompt
via `<world>` tag
- **DESCRIPTION.md** — user-facing app description shown in Slack Home
tab and `/api/info`
- **Any other `.md` at `app/` root** — synced to sandbox at
`/vercel/sandbox/data/` as readable reference docs

This is a clean break with no backward compatibility for ABOUT.md. The
`junior check`
command now errors on deprecated ABOUT.md files and warns on missing
SOUL/WORLD/DESCRIPTION.

### Reference doc sync

Additional `.md` files in the app root (excluding the three reserved
names) are
automatically synced to the Vercel sandbox and listed in the system
prompt's
`<reference-files>` section. Pre-sandbox reads resolve
`/vercel/sandbox/data/*`
paths back to host files so the agent can read references before sandbox
creation.

### Migration for existing apps

1. Rename `app/ABOUT.md` → split into `app/WORLD.md` (operational
context) and `app/DESCRIPTION.md` (user-facing blurb)
2. Run `junior check` to verify

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

Implement debug logging and release verification (#175)

Add structured debug logging across ars-core and related runtime crates, replace library eprintln! diagnostics with log-based warnings, add targeted logging tests and coverage improvements, and document the updated behavior in the foundation specs.

Also fix the ars-i18n release/clippy float-path regression and add a dedicated release verification step in xtask and GitHub Actions so release-mode regressions are exercised in CI.

Closes #147

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

through the root module and CLI. The validator mirrors the existing
formatter and bundle shape, reports missing nested keys and type
mismatches as machine-readable issues, and keeps the CLI proof
side-effect free.

Constraint: Must align with the existing smoke handoff bundle consumed by the formatter

commit.message:
Lock in config leak regressions

Add regression coverage for the stale config metadata scenario from
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/272 and the broader
empty-annotation follow-up path that used to leak prior config state.
The changelog now links #272 to the hidden fallback-state removal
that already fixed the root cause on main.

While verifying with `mise test`, several runtime-specific tests
proved too sensitive to wall-clock timing under the repo's parallel
build-and-test load. Tighten those checks to look for real overlap
where possible and raise a few shell/git test timeouts so the full
suite stays reliable across Deno, Node.js, and Bun.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/272

commit.message:
Hotfix lisibilité des tableaux de segmentation (libellés binaires explicites) (#6)

* Add sponsor-facing Streamlit app, survey analysis pipeline and delivery artifacts

### Motivation
- Finalise a sponsor-ready analysis of the internal AI usage survey and provide reproducible artefacts for decision-making and demo.
- Provide a lightweight Streamlit cockpit for executives with a descriptive/prescriptive toggle and a protected prescriptive mode.
- Fix deployment dependency issues so the Streamlit app boots reliably in the target environment.

### Description
- Adds the data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the exported Excel, normalises columns, computes descriptive stats, runs chi2 tests, generates charts and writes multiple markdown deliverables and `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`.
- Adds a sponsor-facing Streamlit application `app/streamlit_app.py` (plus `app/README.md`) with a global `Description seule` toggle (password-protected prescriptive mode), respondent explorer, segmentations and verbatim thematic views.
- Adds extensive delivery artefacts under `outputs/` (executive summaries, reports, governance/enablement templates, charts, variable dictionary, release notes, demo script, validation and publish status files).
- Fixes Streamlit deployment dependency handling by adding a root `requirements.txt` (includes `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy`) and a UI theme file `.streamlit/config.toml`; sample survey Excel is included under the repo for reproducibility.

### Testing
- Ran `python analyze_survey.py` which completed and printed a JSON `status: ok` (rows=70, raw_cols=91, clean_cols=33); this check succeeded.
- Compiled the app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py`; compilation succeeded.
- Verified Streamlit app startup with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; headless boot was observed and then stopped by the controlled timeout (expected), indicating the app starts without import errors.
- Dependency install check (`pip install -r requirements.txt`) and local import checks passed and resolved previous `scipy` import failures; these validations succeeded.

* Integrate selected COMEX deck narrative into Streamlit app

* Update delivery status for deck integration PR #5

* Fix latest commit in delivery status for PR #5

* Improve segmentation table readability with explicit French binary labels

* Update delivery status for segmentation readability PR #6

* Fix latest commit reference in delivery status for PR #6

commit.message:
Intégration éditoriale du deck COMEX dans l’application Streamlit (#5)

* Add sponsor-facing Streamlit app, survey analysis pipeline and delivery artifacts

### Motivation
- Finalise a sponsor-ready analysis of the internal AI usage survey and provide reproducible artefacts for decision-making and demo.
- Provide a lightweight Streamlit cockpit for executives with a descriptive/prescriptive toggle and a protected prescriptive mode.
- Fix deployment dependency issues so the Streamlit app boots reliably in the target environment.

### Description
- Adds the data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the exported Excel, normalises columns, computes descriptive stats, runs chi2 tests, generates charts and writes multiple markdown deliverables and `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`.
- Adds a sponsor-facing Streamlit application `app/streamlit_app.py` (plus `app/README.md`) with a global `Description seule` toggle (password-protected prescriptive mode), respondent explorer, segmentations and verbatim thematic views.
- Adds extensive delivery artefacts under `outputs/` (executive summaries, reports, governance/enablement templates, charts, variable dictionary, release notes, demo script, validation and publish status files).
- Fixes Streamlit deployment dependency handling by adding a root `requirements.txt` (includes `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy`) and a UI theme file `.streamlit/config.toml`; sample survey Excel is included under the repo for reproducibility.

### Testing
- Ran `python analyze_survey.py` which completed and printed a JSON `status: ok` (rows=70, raw_cols=91, clean_cols=33); this check succeeded.
- Compiled the app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py`; compilation succeeded.
- Verified Streamlit app startup with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; headless boot was observed and then stopped by the controlled timeout (expected), indicating the app starts without import errors.
- Dependency install check (`pip install -r requirements.txt`) and local import checks passed and resolved previous `scipy` import failures; these validations succeeded.

* Integrate selected COMEX deck narrative into Streamlit app

* Update delivery status for deck integration PR #5

* Fix latest commit in delivery status for PR #5

---------

- add an operator-facing guardian-state API surface with confidence, explanation, proof, and next-step data from the canonical guardian state
- wire the cockpit guardian pane to the new surface and render confidence, judgment proof, judgment risks, and guidance instead of only raw observer fields
- extend operator and eval coverage plus shipped-truth docs for the new guardian explanation contract

- keep the operator surface, eval harness, and implementation docs aligned with the real shipped confidence and explanation path

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(sentry): Tighten OAuth setup and dev plugin loading (#179)

Make the example app load the same plugin package set in local dev that
production builds use, and tighten OAuth scope handling for stored user
grants.

The example app was splitting plugin package configuration between Nitro
and `createApp`, which meant local dev could start with a different
plugin set than the production runtime. Separately, stored OAuth tokens
were keyed only by user and provider, so pre-existing Sentry connections
could keep stale scopes and fail later with missing-scope errors after a
seemingly successful reconnect.

This shares the example app plugin package list between Nitro and
`createApp`, keeps the Sentry plugin read-only, persists normalized
granted OAuth scopes with stored tokens, and validates callback,
refresh, and reuse paths against each plugin's required scope.
Under-scoped grants now fail fast and go back through reauthorization
instead of rendering a successful connect flow and failing on the next
tool call.

I considered keeping the reconnect logic only in the Sentry skill, but
that would leave the actual grant validation in the wrong layer and
would still accept obviously insufficient callback or refresh responses.
Validating granted scopes in the OAuth callback and broker keeps the
behavior generic across OAuth-bearer providers while preserving the
Sentry plugin's documented read-only contract.

Split out of the broader `wip/slack-cards-oauth-spike` branch on
purpose. This PR keeps the OAuth and dev-loading fixes separate from the
Slack card experiment.

---------

commit.message:
Hotfix sidebar description toggle and factual/prescriptive navigation (#3)

* Add survey analysis pipeline, sponsor-ready outputs and Streamlit cockpit

### Motivation
- Provide a sponsor-facing package to finalize the IA survey: clean data, produce charts, generate executive deliverables and an interactive Streamlit cockpit for decision-makers.
- Ensure deployability on Streamlit Cloud by declaring runtime dependencies and a UI theme for consistent presentation.
- Capture traceable artifacts (analysis report, one-pager, roadmap, governance, enablement plan, charts and cleaned CSV) for audit and review.

### Description
- Added a data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the provided Excel survey, normalizes column names, produces `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, generates charts under `outputs/charts/`, builds `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv` and writes multiple deliverables (`analysis_report.md`, `executive_summary.md`, `one_pager_comex.md`, `board_deck_outline.md`, etc.).
- Added a sponsor-facing Streamlit app in `app/streamlit_app.py` with `app/README.md`, using the generated `outputs` artifacts as sources and a custom theme in `.streamlit/config.toml` for consistent styling.
- Added runtime dependency management via `requirements.txt` including `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy` to ensure the app boots in Streamlit Cloud and local environments.
- Included the raw survey file and numerous `outputs/*.md` artifacts (diagnostics, delivery status, reviewer guide, roadmap, enablement plan, governance framework, demo script, etc.) to make the analysis and publication state explicit and reviewable.

### Testing
- Executed the analysis pipeline via `python analyze_survey.py`, which completed and printed a JSON status indicating `"status": "ok"` and `rows=70`, `raw_cols=91`, `clean_cols=33` (success).
- Compiled the Streamlit app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py` with no syntax errors (OK).
- Launched Streamlit headless to validate boot with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; startup was observed and stopped by the timeout, with no `ModuleNotFoundError` for `scipy` after adding `requirements.txt` (OK).
- Verified existence and sizes of key output artifacts and charts (`outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, `outputs/executive_summary.md`, `outputs/analysis_report.md`, `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv`, `outputs/charts/*.png`) as part of the final quality checks (OK).

* Implement phase 3 factual/recommendation navigation and artifacts

* Update delivery status with verified PR and validation details

* Fix latest commit hash in delivery status

* Sync delivery status with current latest commit

* Make delivery status latest-commit field verification-based

* Hotfix sidebar Description seule navigation and status artifacts
